articles.summary: Without trust, transparency, and strong privacy practices, innovation can backfire. As third-party cookies disappear and privacy regulations expand, marketers looking to build on the latest AI technologies must invest in first-party data.

articles.title: OK BOOMER, WHAT'S NEXT? RETHINKING GENERATIONAL MARKETING MYTHS
articles.summary: Generational marketing based on birth-year labels is oversimplified. Instead, focus on factors like life stage, identity, fandoms, microcultures, and behavioral mindsets to understand real consumer motivations. Segmentation works best when it blends cultural nuance, data, and psychographics rather than relying on clichés. Generational cohorts can provide context, but shouldn't be the main lens. Marketing today requires interest-driven and culturally attuned approaches to reflect how people actually live.

articles.title: WHAT AI MARKETING JOBS LOOK LIKE TODAY
articles.summary: AI has become a central part of marketing jobs, with many listings now requiring AI skills or experience. About 75% of workers report using AI because it is officially or unofficially expected, yet 95% of genAI programs at work are failing. New roles such as AI marketing program managers at large companies like Stripe pay up to $210,000, while startups like Firecrawl are experimenting with fully AI-driven agent roles offering $5,000 monthly retainers. The future of marketing jobs is splitting into three paths: fully AI-focused roles, traditional roles adapted with AI expectations, and fully AI agents replacing human work, leaving nearly 60% of marketers worried about job replacement.

articles.title: WIN REPORT: REMEMBER ME? HOW SAVED SESSIONS BOOSTED CONVERSIONS BY 16%
articles.summary: A travel booking platform tested a “welcome back” prompt that reminded users of the last experience they viewed. The prompt helped visitors continue their browsing without restarting, which addressed a major friction point in vacation planning. The test showed a 16% increase in conversions among returning visitors. The results highlight the importance of continuity in multi-session decision-making.

articles.title: FROM QUERY TO CITATION: HOW SNIPPET SIGNALS INFLUENCE AI SEARCH
articles.summary: AI search engines reward content that aligns with user intent and search language. Informational queries need precision, with 70% of cited pages matching at least 60% of query terms and over 60% using phrases like “how to” or “what is.” Commercial queries favor breadth, with 25% of titles using synonyms and 90% having under 30% word overlap. To improve visibility, match titles and slugs to user intent, keep phrasing clear for informational searches, and use synonyms and related terms for commercial ones.

articles.title: HOW TO RUN GMAIL ADS IN 2025
articles.summary: Google has reintroduced Gmail Ads through Demand Gen campaigns, allowing advertisers to run campaigns exclusively in Gmail. Gmail Ads can appear as single image, carousel, video, or product feed ads, and they show in the Promotions or Social tabs with a teaser ad that expands into a full ad when clicked. Targeting options include demographics, in-market segments, custom segments, lookalikes, and retargeting, though content targeting is not available. Success is measured through engagement rate, click-through rate, and conversion rate, with strategies varying depending on whether the audience is warm or cold.

articles.title: CUSTOM GPT TIP
articles.summary: Use “input flows” when building custom GPTs to improve output quality. Instead of giving one big prompt, break the task into a series of structured questions, each with predefined answer options. For example, when creating a content marketing GPT, ask about audience familiarity, topic, primary audience, and key messages step by step. This approach mimics a human interviewer, ensuring the GPT receives a clear, detailed brief so it can deliver better results.

articles.title: LISTEN LATER
articles.summary: Listen Later converts written content, such as articles, blog posts, PDFs, newsletters, and email text, into audio podcast episodes. The service cleans up the content, removes ads, converts it to audio, and delivers it to a private podcast feed that works in any standard podcast app for listening on the go.

articles.title: HUBSPOT UNVEILS LOOP MARKETING STRATEGY TO ADDRESS AI DISRUPTION
articles.summary: 60% of searches now end on platforms like Google or ChatGPT. HubSpot launched “the Loop” to address shrinking website traffic and evolving customer journeys. The framework consists of four stages (Express, Tailor, Amplify, and Evolve) that accelerate campaign launches, improve personalization, and boost performance through AI-assisted iteration.

articles.title: WILL GOOGLE'S ANTITRUST SEARCH RULING IMPACT MARKETING SEO STRATEGIES?
articles.summary: Google will be required to share parts of its search index and user-interaction data with qualified competitors, including AI platforms such as ChatGPT and Perplexity, marking a major regulatory milestone in the search market. While immediate effects on rankings may be limited because core algorithms and advertising data remain under Google's control, the decision signals a longer-term shift toward a more fragmented search ecosystem as Google's market share drops below 90% and AI-driven assistants gain momentum. For marketers, this creates a future where visibility will depend on building authority, securing placement across multiple discovery platforms, and optimizing for both search engines and AI assistants.

articles.title: MOST MARKETERS ARE FRAUDS
articles.summary: Every marketer should do a rotation in customer support before touching a campaign.

articles.title: DON'T SHIP A PRODUCT WITHOUT A BETA
articles.summary: Don't launch a product or a campaign without first running an alpha or beta test.

articles.title: A DEMO OF VEED FABRIC 1.0
articles.summary: Sections in the video marked with “Generated with VEED Fabric 1.0” are AI-generated, making it easy to distinguish human from AI parts.
